Package chess

Class King

java.lang.Object
chess.Board
chess.King
public class King extends Board
The King class represents a king chess piece on a chess board. It extends the Board class and inherits its methods and properties.
Author:
Pavitra Patel, Huzaif Mansuri

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static boolean
    blcast
    Indicates if the black left castling is still possible.
    static boolean
    brcast
    Indicates if the black right castling is still possible.
    static boolean
    wlcast
    Indicates if the white left castling is still possible.
    static boolean
    wrcast
    Indicates if the white right castling is still possible.

      public static boolean wlcast
      Indicates if the white left castling is still possible.

      Indicates if the white right castling is still possible.

      Indicates if the black left castling is still possible.

      Indicates if the black right castling is still possible.

  • Constructor Details

    • King

      public King()
      Creates a new King object with default values for color and name.

  • Method Details

    • isValid

      public boolean isValid(Board[][] board, int initiali, int initialj, int finali, int finalj)
      Checks if a move from the initial position to the final position of the king is valid.
      Specified by:
      isValid in class Board
      Parameters:
      board - the chess board as a 2D array of Board objects
      initiali - the initial column index of the king on the board
      initialj - the initial row index of the king on the board
      finali - the final column index of the king on the board
      finalj - the final row index of the king on the board
      Returns:
      true if the move is valid, false otherwise

    • move

      public Board move(Board obj)
      Creates a new King object with the same color and name as the current King object.
      Specified by:
      move in class Board
      Parameters:
      obj - the Board object to move the King to
      Returns:
      a new King object with the same color and name as the current King object

    • setColor

      public void setColor(String c)
      Sets the color of the King object.
      Specified by:
      setColor in class Board
      Parameters:
      c - a String representing the color of the King object ("w" for white, "b" for black)

    • setName

      public void setName(String n)
      Sets the name of the King object.
      Specified by:
      setName in class Board
      Parameters:
      n - a String representing the name of the King object ("K")

    • getColor

      public String getColor()
      Gets the color of the King object.
      Specified by:
      getColor in class Board
      Returns:
      a String representing the color of the King object ("w" for white, "b" for black)

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Gets the name of the King object.
      Specified by:
      getName in class Board
      Returns:
      a String representing the name of the King object ("K")
